inclusion_criteria: Adult humans with chronic kidney disease (CKD) and treatment-resistant hypertension (as defined by study or standard criteria)
Intervention: renal denervation using endovascular catheter-based radiofrequency ablation as the sole antihypertensive procedure
Primary clinical studies (randomized/nonrandomized trials or observational cohorts) that include within-participant pre- and post-intervention measurements
Reports at least one outcome: office or ambulatory BP (SBP/DBP), eGFR, serum creatinine, or procedural complication rates
exclusion_criteria: Not a primary clinical study or lacks baseline comparator (e.g., reviews, editorials, case reports, cross-sectional without follow-up, or no pre/post data)
Population not adult CKD with treatment-resistant hypertension or CKD/TRH subgroup data not extractable
Intervention not radiofrequency endovascular RDN alone (e.g., ultrasound-based RDN, chemical or surgical denervation, or combined procedures without separable RDN effects)
Does not report any prespecified outcomes (BP, eGFR, serum creatinine, or procedural complications)
screening_criteria.description: Screen studies evaluating radiofrequency catheter-based renal denervation in adults with CKD and treatment-resistant hypertension, requiring pre/post data and at least one specified clinical or safety outcome.

included_articles.title: Renal denervation preserves renal function in patients with chronic kidney disease and resistant hypertension.
Insight on Efficacy of Renal Artery Denervation for Refractory Hypertension with Chronic Kidney Diseases: A Long-Term Follow-Up of 24-Hour Ambulatory Blood Pressure.
Renal denervation in moderate to severe CKD.
Insights on safety and efficacy of renal artery denervation for uncontrolled-resistant hypertension in a high risk population with chronic kidney disease: first Italian real-world experience.
Renal denervation in patients with end-stage renal disease and resistant hypertension on long-term haemodialysis.
[Ultra-long-term follow-up of renal denervation in patients with resistant hypertension and mild chronic kidney disease].
